gist.headline: Making a splash
gist.blurb: Splash Tool just landed in the greenhouse! Now you can slice and dice screenshots into editable layers, extracting colors and assets like a digital botanist.
gist.summary: Added Splash Tool to the community apps showcase, a new utility for converting screenshots into editable layers and extracting design assets.
gist.impact: Users can now easily deconstruct screenshots to extract colors, layouts, and assets for their own design workflows.
gist.visual_concept: Splash Tool extracts layers and colors from screenshots. Visual metaphor: separating an image into floating layers or splashing colors. Polly the bee wearing a painter's beret, working with the Monitor Robot who is displaying a layered image.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny painter's beret, flying next to a chunky retro monitor robot. The robot is holding a glowing screenshot that is magically separating into floating, colorful pixel layers. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background. Chunky retro sprites. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
